titleStmt.title: SII 28.202: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 202. (A.R. No. 357 of 1913.) CHIDAMBARAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: Naṭarāja temple, west gōpuram. right of entrance.

Contains an ode-like verse in Tamil lamenting the demise of a Pāṇḍya king, who seems to have died in Kūḍal (Madurai). He is stated to have been a Māṟaṉ and credited with the legendary feat of a bygone legendary progenitor of the Pāṇḍyas who is said to have hit the Vāsava (Indra, the king of the dēvas) on his head with his vāṇḍai (cf. verse 7 in the larger Śiṉṉamaṉūr Plates-SII, Vol. III, p. 451 and 457).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
vaṇṭai vācavaṉ ceṉṉiyilocciya māṟaṉ ve
la valla vaiyyantoḻuta One letter ka seems to have been written here and then cut off.van koṇṭalāḷun to
ṭar maruvār peṟak koṭutta koṟṟavaṉ kūṭaṟ ci
lampile yaṇṭārvi taḷarumpaṭi vanta vālakāla
maṭaṅkalum vantoru keṇṭai yātoru potiṟ ki
ṭantatu kiṭakkume yiṉi keṭṭeṉi vaiyyame §
Digital edition of SII 28.202 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
